evidence_quote: rs12913832 alone carries most of the eye-color predictive information and is the best-known predictor: among 49 CC (rs12913832) individuals, 45 (91.8%) had blue eyes
classification_notes: HERC2 canonical; IrisPlex panel marker, mechanism cited from prior literature only.
author_explanation_quote: It is assumed that the level of expression of the known pigmentation gene – OCA2 – is controlled by polymorphism rs12913832 on HERC2 locus

evidence_quote: rs1800407 (second-ranked predictor) had low allele-A frequency (11.9%), limiting its influence.
author_explanation_quote: The remaining genes that have been shown to contribute to eye color variation are SLC24A4, SLC45A2, TYR, and IRF4...their impact on eye color prediction is lower and it seems to vary between populations
notes: This is a validation study of the established IrisPlex panel, not a discovery paper; the SNP is cited as a known predictor gene with no locus-specific mechanistic account given IN THIS PAPER (mechanism, if any, resides in the cited prior literature). Classified nearest_gene_only as the closest controlled-vocabulary match for 'no mechanism stated by these authors', though the gene label is an established panel-marker assignment, not a positional nearest-gene call.

evidence_quote: Six IrisPlex predictor SNPs (HERC2 rs12913832, OCA2 rs1800407, SLC45A2 rs16891982, TYR rs1393350, SLC24A4 rs12896399, and IRF4 rs12203592) of 105 individuals were analyzed using single base extension approach and SNaPshot chemistry.
